"""
=========================================================
Voice Selector
---------------------------------------------------------
Speech AI Platform

Responsável por selecionar automaticamente o VoiceProfile
mais adequado para um idioma previamente detectado.

Fluxo
-----
Language
    ↓
VoiceManager
    ↓
VoiceProfile

Responsabilidades
-----------------
- Selecionar o VoiceProfile padrão
- Validar disponibilidade da voz
- Garantir compatibilidade com o Provider configurado

Author: Rodrigo Magalhães
=========================================================
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging

from config.config_manager import ConfigManager
from models import Language
from models import VoiceProfile

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class VoiceSelector:
    """
    Serviço responsável por selecionar o VoiceProfile
    mais adequado para um idioma.
    """

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def __init__(
        self,
        cfg: ConfigManager
    ) -> None:
        """
        Inicializa o Voice Selector.

        Parameters
        ----------
        cfg : ConfigManager
            Configuração global da aplicação.
        """

        self.cfg = cfg

        self.voice_manager = cfg.voice_manager

        logger.info(
            "VoiceSelector initialized."
        )

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def select(
        self,
        language: Language
    ) -> VoiceProfile:
        """
        Seleciona automaticamente um VoiceProfile.

        Parameters
        ----------
        language : Language
            Idioma previamente detectado.

        Returns
        -------
        VoiceProfile
            Perfil de voz selecionado.

        Raises
        ------
        TypeError
            Caso o parâmetro não seja um objeto Language.

        ValueError
            Caso não exista um VoiceProfile compatível.
        """

        if not isinstance(language, Language):

            raise TypeError(

                "language must be an instance of Language."

            )

        logger.info(

            "Selecting voice for language '%s' using provider '%s'.",

            language.code,

            self.cfg.provider

        )

        profile = self.voice_manager.get_default_by_language(

            language=language.code,

            provider=self.cfg.provider

        )

        if profile is None:

            logger.error(

                "No VoiceProfile available for language '%s' and provider '%s'.",

                language.code,

                self.cfg.provider

            )

            raise ValueError(

                f"No VoiceProfile found for language "

                f"'{language.code}'."

            )

        logger.info(

            "Voice selected successfully: %s (%s)",

            profile.voice,

            profile.provider

        )

        return profile

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def __call__(
        self,
        language: Language
    ) -> VoiceProfile:
        """
        Permite utilizar o objeto como função.

        Example
        -------
        profile = selector(language)
        """

        return self.select(language)

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def __repr__(
        self
    ) -> str:

        return (

            "VoiceSelector("

            f"provider='{self.cfg.provider}')"

        )

"""
=========================================================
Speech Optimizer
---------------------------------------------------------
Speech AI Platform

Converte um SpeechAnalysis em um SpeechProfile.

Enterprise Version 2.1

Author: Rodrigo Magalhães
=========================================================
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from time import perf_counter

from models.speech_profile import SpeechProfile
from services.text_optimizer import TextOptimizer

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class SpeechOptimizer:
    """
    Responsável por transformar um SpeechAnalysis
    em um SpeechProfile otimizado para síntese.

    Também disponibiliza uma camada de otimização
    textual que prepara o roteiro para o mecanismo
    TTS, mantendo independência do Provider.
    """

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def __init__(self):

        #
        # Engine responsável pela preparação do texto
        # para síntese de voz.
        #
        self.text_optimizer = TextOptimizer()

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def optimize(
        self,
        analysis,
        voice_profile
    ) -> SpeechProfile:

        start = perf_counter()

        logger.info("=" * 60)
        logger.info("Speech Optimizer")
        logger.info("=" * 60)

        profile = self._create_profile()

        self._apply_voice(
            profile,
            voice_profile
        )

        self._apply_analysis(
            profile,
            analysis
        )

        self._apply_rate(
            profile,
            analysis
        )

        self._apply_pitch(
            profile,
            analysis
        )

        self._apply_reading_style(
            profile,
            analysis
        )

        self._apply_pacing(
            profile,
            analysis
        )

        self._calculate_confidence(
            profile,
            analysis
        )

        #
        # Hook para futuras regras baseadas em IA.
        #
        self._apply_future_ai_rules(
            profile,
            analysis
        )

        elapsed = round(
            (perf_counter() - start) * 1000,
            2
        )

        self._log_profile(
            profile,
            elapsed
        )

        return profile

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def optimize_text(
        self,
        text: str
    ) -> str:
        """
        Executa a otimização textual antes da síntese.

        Atualmente aplica:

        - limpeza
        - normalização
        - preparação para leitura

        Futuras versões:

        - SSML
        - IA Generativa
        - Storytelling
        - Emotion Engine
        - Pause Engine
        """

        logger.info("=" * 60)
        logger.info("Text Optimization")
        logger.info("=" * 60)

        optimized = self.text_optimizer.optimize(text)

        logger.info(
            "Characters: %d -> %d",
            len(text),
            len(optimized)
        )

        logger.info("=" * 60)

        return optimized

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _create_profile(self):

        return SpeechProfile()

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _apply_voice(
        self,
        profile,
        voice_profile
    ):

        profile.voice = voice_profile.voice
        profile.locale = voice_profile.locale
        profile.provider = voice_profile.provider

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _apply_analysis(
        self,
        profile,
        analysis
    ):

        profile.language = analysis.language

        profile.complexity = analysis.complexity

        profile.estimated_minutes = analysis.estimated_minutes

        profile.recommended_wpm = analysis.recommended_wpm

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _apply_rate(
        self,
        profile,
        analysis
    ):

        profile.rate = analysis.recommended_rate

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _apply_pitch(
        self,
        profile,
        analysis
    ):

        complexity = str(
            analysis.complexity
        ).upper()

        if complexity == "HIGH":

            profile.pitch = "-1Hz"

        elif complexity == "MEDIUM":

            profile.pitch = "+0Hz"

        else:

            profile.pitch = "+1Hz"

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _apply_reading_style(
        self,
        profile,
        analysis
    ):

        avg = analysis.average_words_per_sentence

        if avg >= 20:

            profile.reading_style = "Technical"

        elif avg >= 14:

            profile.reading_style = "Business"

        else:

            profile.reading_style = "Conversational"

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _apply_pacing(
        self,
        profile,
        analysis
    ):

        complexity = str(
            analysis.complexity
        ).upper()

        if complexity == "HIGH":

            profile.pacing = "Slow"

        elif complexity == "MEDIUM":

            profile.pacing = "Normal"

        else:

            profile.pacing = "Fast"

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _calculate_confidence(
        self,
        profile,
        analysis
    ):

        confidence = 1.0

        #
        # Texto muito pequeno
        #

        if analysis.words < 150:

            confidence -= 0.10

        #
        # Poucas frases
        #

        if analysis.sentences < 8:

            confidence -= 0.10

        #
        # Diversidade lexical
        #

        if hasattr(
            analysis,
            "vocabulary_density"
        ):

            if analysis.vocabulary_density < 0.35:

                confidence -= 0.05

        #
        # Confiança do detector de idioma
        #

        if hasattr(
            analysis,
            "language_confidence"
        ):

            if analysis.language_confidence < 0.80:

                confidence -= 0.05

        profile.confidence = round(

            max(
                confidence,
                0.0
            ),

            2

        )

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _apply_future_ai_rules(
        self,
        profile,
        analysis
    ):

        """
        Hook reservado para Sprint 9.

        Engines previstas:

        - Azure OpenAI
        - OpenAI GPT
        - Claude
        - Gemini
        - Ollama
        - Emotion Engine
        - Storytelling Engine
        """

        return

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _log_profile(
        self,
        profile,
        elapsed
    ):

        logger.info("=" * 60)
        logger.info("Speech Profile")
        logger.info("=" * 60)

        logger.info(
            "Language.......... %s",
            profile.language
        )

        logger.info(
            "Voice............. %s",
            profile.voice
        )

        logger.info(
            "Provider.......... %s",
            profile.provider
        )

        logger.info(
            "Rate.............. %s",
            profile.rate
        )

        logger.info(
            "Pitch............. %s",
            profile.pitch
        )

        logger.info(
            "Reading Style..... %s",
            profile.reading_style
        )

        logger.info(
            "Pacing............ %s",
            profile.pacing
        )

        logger.info(
            "Complexity........ %s",
            profile.complexity
        )

        logger.info(
            "Confidence........ %.2f",
            profile.confidence
        )

        logger.info(
            "Optimization Time. %.2f ms",
            elapsed
        )

        logger.info("=" * 60)
from services.text_optimizer import TextOptimizer

"""
=========================================================
Speech Service
---------------------------------------------------------
Speech AI Platform

Responsável exclusivamente pela síntese de voz.

Fluxo

Narration File
        │
        ▼
SpeechProfile
        │
        ▼
Provider Factory
        │
        ▼
TTS Engine
        │
        ▼
Audio

Author: Rodrigo Magalhães
=========================================================
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from pathlib import Path

from models.speech_profile import SpeechProfile

from providers.provider_factory import ProviderFactory

from services.tts_engine import TTSEngine

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class SpeechService:

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def __init__(self):

        pass

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def synthesize(

        self,

        speech_profile: SpeechProfile,

        narration_file: Path,

        output_file: Path

    ) -> Path:

        logger.info("=" * 60)
        logger.info("Speech Service")
        logger.info("=" * 60)

        narration_file = Path(narration_file)

        if not narration_file.exists():

            raise FileNotFoundError(

                f"Narration file not found:\n{narration_file}"

            )

        text = narration_file.read_text(
            encoding="utf-8"
        )

        logger.info(
            "Characters: %d",
            len(text)
        )

        # =====================================================
        # Provider
        # =====================================================

        provider = ProviderFactory.create_from_speech_profile(

            speech_profile

        )

        logger.info(
            "Provider: %s",
            provider
        )

        # =====================================================
        # Engine
        # =====================================================

        engine = TTSEngine(
            provider
        )

        audio = engine.generate(

            text=text,

            output_path=output_file

        )

        logger.info("Speech synthesis completed.")

        return audio

"""
=========================================================
Timing Calculator
---------------------------------------------------------
Speech AI Platform

Calcula automaticamente o ritmo ideal da narração.

Author: Rodrigo Magalhães
=========================================================
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class TimingCalculator:

    def calculate(self, analysis):

        logger.info("Calculating speech timing...")

        average = analysis.average_words_per_sentence

        # -------------------------------
        # Complexidade
        # -------------------------------

        if average >= 22:

            complexity = "High"

            wpm = 130

            rate = "-15%"

        elif average >= 16:

            complexity = "Medium"

            wpm = 140

            rate = "-10%"

        else:

            complexity = "Low"

            wpm = 150

            rate = "-5%"

        # -------------------------------
        # Ajuste por idioma
        # -------------------------------

        if analysis.language == "pt":

            wpm -= 5

        # -------------------------------
        # Atualiza o objeto
        # -------------------------------

        analysis.complexity = complexity

        analysis.recommended_wpm = wpm

        analysis.recommended_rate = rate

        logger.info(

            "Complexity..... %s",

            complexity

        )

        logger.info(

            "Recommended WPM %s",

            wpm

        )

        logger.info(

            "Recommended Rate %s",

            rate

        )

        return analysis

"""
=========================================================
TTS Engine
---------------------------------------------------------
Speech AI Platform

Responsável por executar a síntese de voz utilizando
qualquer Provider compatível com BaseTTSProvider.

Este componente desacopla os Services da implementação
dos Providers.

Fluxo:

SpeechService
        │
        ▼
TTSEngine
        │
        ▼
Provider
        │
        ▼
Audio

Author: Rodrigo Magalhães
=========================================================
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from pathlib import Path

from providers.base_provider import BaseTTSProvider

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class TTSEngine:
    """
    Engine responsável pela execução da síntese de voz.
    """

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def __init__(
        self,
        provider: BaseTTSProvider
    ):

        self.provider = provider

        logger.info(
            "TTS Engine initialized with provider: %s",
            provider
        )

    # -------------------------------------------------

    @property
    def provider_name(self) -> str:
        """
        Nome do provider atualmente utilizado.
        """

        return self.provider.__class__.__name__

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def generate(
        self,
        text: str,
        output_path: Path
    ) -> Path:
        """
        Executa a geração do áudio.

        Parameters
        ----------
        text
            Texto que será sintetizado.

        output_path
            Caminho do arquivo MP3.

        Returns
        -------
        Path
            Caminho do áudio gerado.
        """

        logger.info("=" * 60)
        logger.info("TTS Engine")
        logger.info("=" * 60)

        logger.info(
            "Provider.....: %s",
            self.provider_name
        )

        logger.info(
            "Characters...: %d",
            len(text)
        )

        logger.info(
            "Output.......: %s",
            output_path
        )

        audio = self.provider.generate(
            text=text,
            output_path=output_path
        )

        logger.info("TTS finished successfully.")

        return audio

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def __str__(self) -> str:

        return (
            f"TTSEngine("
            f"{self.provider_name}"
            f")"
        )

"""
=========================================================
Speech Analyzer
---------------------------------------------------------
Speech AI Platform

Analisa o roteiro antes da síntese.

Enterprise Pipeline Version 2.0

Author: Rodrigo Magalhães
=========================================================
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import re
import logging
from time import perf_counter

from models.speech_analysis import SpeechAnalysis

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class SpeechAnalyzer:
    """
    Pipeline de análise avançada de roteiro.
    """

    DEFAULT_WPM = 145

    # =========================================================
    # PUBLIC API
    # =========================================================

    def analyze(
        self,
        presentation,
        language
    ) -> SpeechAnalysis:

        start = perf_counter()

        logger.info("=" * 60)
        logger.info("Speech Analyzer Started")
        logger.info("=" * 60)

        text = self._extract_text(presentation)

        analysis = SpeechAnalysis()

        analysis.language = language.code

        stats = presentation.statistics

        # -----------------------------
        # Basic Metrics
        # -----------------------------
        self._load_basic_metrics(analysis, stats, presentation)

        # -----------------------------
        # Text Processing
        # -----------------------------
        words = self._extract_words(text)

        sentences = stats.sentences

        # -----------------------------
        # Advanced Analysis
        # -----------------------------
        self._analyze_patterns(analysis, text, words)

        self._calculate_word_metrics(analysis, words)

        self._calculate_sentence_metrics(analysis, words, sentences)

        self._calculate_advanced_metrics(analysis, words)

        # -----------------------------
        # Final Metrics
        # -----------------------------
        analysis.lexical_diversity = self._lexical_diversity(words)

        analysis.execution_time_ms = round(
            (perf_counter() - start) * 1000,
            2
        )

        # -----------------------------
        # Logging Enterprise
        # -----------------------------
        self._log_analysis(analysis)

        logger.info("Speech Analyzer Completed")

        return analysis

    # =========================================================
    # PIPELINE METHODS
    # =========================================================

    def _extract_text(self, presentation) -> str:
        """
        Extrai texto da apresentação.
        """

        if hasattr(presentation, "to_text"):

            return presentation.to_text()

        raise AttributeError(
            "Presentation object must implement to_text()"
        )

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _load_basic_metrics(
        self,
        analysis,
        stats,
        presentation
    ) -> None:

        analysis.words = stats.words
        analysis.characters = stats.characters
        analysis.sentences = stats.sentences
        analysis.paragraphs = stats.paragraphs
        analysis.estimated_minutes = stats.estimated_minutes
        analysis.slides = presentation.total_slides

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _extract_words(self, text: str) -> list[str]:

        return re.findall(r"\b[\w'-]+\b", text)

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _analyze_patterns(
        self,
        analysis,
        text: str,
        words: list[str]
    ) -> None:

        analysis.list_items = len(
            re.findall(r"^[-•*]", text, re.MULTILINE)
        )

        analysis.numbers = len(
            re.findall(r"\d+", text)
        )

        analysis.uppercase_words = len([
            w for w in words
            if len(w) > 1 and w.isupper()
        ])

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _calculate_word_metrics(
        self,
        analysis,
        words: list[str]
    ) -> None:

        if analysis.words > 0:

            analysis.average_word_length = round(
                sum(len(w) for w in words) / analysis.words,
                1
            )

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _calculate_sentence_metrics(
        self,
        analysis,
        words: list[str],
        sentences: int
    ) -> None:

        if sentences > 0:

            analysis.average_words_per_sentence = round(
                len(words) / sentences,
                1
            )

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _calculate_advanced_metrics(
        self,
        analysis,
        words: list[str]
    ) -> None:

        unique_words = set(w.lower() for w in words)

        analysis.unique_words = len(unique_words)

        analysis.vocabulary_density = round(
            len(unique_words) / len(words),
            2
        ) if words else 0.0

        analysis.complexity = self._calculate_complexity(
            analysis.vocabulary_density,
            analysis.average_words_per_sentence
        )

        analysis.reading_style = self._detect_style(
            analysis.average_words_per_sentence
        )

        analysis.reading_pace = self._detect_pace(
            analysis.average_words_per_sentence
        )

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _lexical_diversity(self, words: list[str]) -> float:

        if not words:
            return 0.0

        return len(set(words)) / len(words)

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _calculate_complexity(
        self,
        diversity: float,
        avg_sentence_length: float
    ) -> str:

        score = diversity * avg_sentence_length

        if score < 5:
            return "LOW"

        if score < 10:
            return "MEDIUM"

        return "HIGH"

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _detect_style(
        self,
        avg_sentence_length: float
    ) -> str:

        if avg_sentence_length < 10:
            return "Conversational"

        if avg_sentence_length < 18:
            return "Presentation"

        return "Technical"

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _detect_pace(
        self,
        avg_sentence_length: float
    ) -> str:

        if avg_sentence_length < 10:
            return "Fast"

        if avg_sentence_length < 18:
            return "Normal"

        return "Slow"

    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    def _log_analysis(
        self,
        analysis
    ) -> None:

        logger.info("=" * 60)
        logger.info("Speech Analysis Report")
        logger.info("=" * 60)

        logger.info("Words.............. %s", analysis.words)
        logger.info("Sentences.......... %s", analysis.sentences)
        logger.info("Paragraphs......... %s", analysis.paragraphs)
        logger.info("Characters......... %s", analysis.characters)

        logger.info("List Items......... %s", analysis.list_items)
        logger.info("Numbers............ %s", analysis.numbers)
        logger.info("Uppercase Words.... %s", analysis.uppercase_words)

        logger.info("Vocabulary Density.. %.2f", analysis.vocabulary_density)
        logger.info("Complexity......... %s", analysis.complexity)
        logger.info("Reading Style...... %s", analysis.reading_style)
        logger.info("Reading Pace....... %s", analysis.reading_pace)

        logger.info("Execution Time..... %sms", analysis.execution_time_ms)

        logger.info("=" * 60)

"""
=========================================================
Language Detection Service
---------------------------------------------------------
Speech AI Platform

Responsável por detectar automaticamente o idioma
predominante de um texto utilizando detecção offline.

Responsabilidades
-----------------
- Detectar idioma predominante
- Normalizar textos
- Cachear resultados
- Disponibilizar estatísticas
- Servir como serviço central de detecção

Author: Rodrigo Magalhães
=========================================================
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from pathlib import Path
from time import perf_counter

from langdetect import DetectorFactory
from langdetect import detect_langs

from models import Language

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# Garante resultados determinísticos
DetectorFactory.seed = 0


class LanguageDetector:
    """
    Serviço responsável pela detecção automática do idioma
    predominante de um texto.
    """

    # -------------------------------------------------

    _LANGUAGE_MAP = {

        "pt": Language(
            code="pt",
            locale="pt-BR",
            name="Portuguese"
        ),

        "en": Language(
            code="en",
            locale="en-US",
            name="English"
        ),

        "es": Language(
            code="es",
            locale="es-ES",
            name="Spanish"
        ),

        "fr": Language(
            code="fr",
            locale="fr-FR",
            name="French"
        ),

    }

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def __init__(self) -> None:

        self._cache: dict[int, Language] = {}

        self._statistics = {

            "detections": 0,
            "cache_hits": 0

        }

        logger.info(
            "LanguageDetector initialized."
        )

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def _normalize(
        self,
        text: str
    ) -> str:

        if not isinstance(text, str):

            raise TypeError(
                "text must be a string."
            )

        text = text.strip()

        if not text:

            raise ValueError(
                "Cannot detect language from empty text."
            )

        return text

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def _cache_key(
        self,
        text: str
    ) -> int:

        return hash(text)

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def detect(
        self,
        text: str
    ) -> Language:
        """
        Detecta o idioma predominante.

        Parameters
        ----------
        text : str

        Returns
        -------
        Language
        """

        start = perf_counter()

        text = self._normalize(text)

        key = self._cache_key(text)

        if key in self._cache:

            self._statistics["cache_hits"] += 1

            logger.info(
                "Language detection cache hit."
            )

            return self._cache[key]

        logger.info(
            "Starting language detection..."
        )

        logger.info(
            "Characters: %d",
            len(text)
        )

        try:

            result = detect_langs(text)[0]

        except Exception:

            logger.exception(
                "Language detection failed."
            )

            raise

        code = result.lang.lower()

        confidence = result.prob

        base = self._LANGUAGE_MAP.get(code)

        if base is None:

            logger.warning(
                "Unsupported language detected: %s",
                code
            )

            language = Language(

                code=code,

                locale=code,

                name="Unknown",

                confidence=confidence

            )

        else:

            language = Language(

                code=base.code,

                locale=base.locale,

                name=base.name,

                confidence=confidence

            )

        self._cache[key] = language

        self._statistics["detections"] += 1

        elapsed = (
            perf_counter() - start
        ) * 1000

        logger.info(
            "Language detected: %s",
            language.name
        )

        logger.info(
            "Confidence: %.2f%%",
            confidence * 100
        )

        logger.info(
            "Detection time: %.2f ms",
            elapsed
        )

        return language

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def detect_file(
        self,
        file_path: str | Path
    ) -> Language:
        """
        Detecta o idioma de um arquivo texto.
        """

        file_path = Path(file_path)

        logger.info(
            "Reading file: %s",
            file_path
        )

        with open(
            file_path,
            encoding="utf-8"
        ) as fp:

            return self.detect(
                fp.read()
            )

    # -------------------------------------------------

    @property
    def statistics(
        self
    ) -> dict[str, int]:

        return {

            "detections":
                self._statistics["detections"],

            "cache_hits":
                self._statistics["cache_hits"],

            "cache_size":
                len(self._cache)

        }

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def clear_cache(
        self
    ) -> None:

        logger.info(
            "Clearing language cache."
        )

        self._cache.clear()

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def __repr__(
        self
    ) -> str:

        return (

            "LanguageDetector("

            f"detections={self._statistics['detections']}, "

            f"cache={len(self._cache)})"

        )

"""
=========================================================
Speech Intelligence Engine
---------------------------------------------------------
Speech AI Platform

Centraliza toda a inteligência da narração.

Fluxo:

Presentation
      │
      ▼
Language Detection
      │
      ▼
Voice Selection
      │
      ▼
Speech Analysis
      │
      ▼
Timing Calculation
      │
      ▼
Speech Optimization
      │
      ▼
SpeechProfile

Author: Rodrigo Magalhães
=========================================================
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging

from config.config_manager import ConfigManager

from services.language_detector import LanguageDetector
from services.voice_selector import VoiceSelector
from services.speech_analyzer import SpeechAnalyzer
from services.timing_calculator import TimingCalculator
from services.speech_optimizer import SpeechOptimizer

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class SpeechIntelligenceEngine:

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def __init__(
        self,
        cfg: ConfigManager
    ):

        self.cfg = cfg

        self.language_detector = LanguageDetector()

        self.voice_selector = VoiceSelector(cfg)

        self.speech_analyzer = SpeechAnalyzer()

        self.timing_calculator = TimingCalculator()

        self.speech_optimizer = SpeechOptimizer()

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def build_profile(
        self,
        presentation
    ):

        logger.info("=" * 60)
        logger.info("Speech Intelligence Engine")
        logger.info("=" * 60)

        text = presentation.to_text()

        # ------------------------------------------
        # Language
        # ------------------------------------------

        language = self.language_detector.detect(
            text
        )

        logger.info(
            "Language detected: %s",
            language.code
        )

        # ------------------------------------------
        # Voice
        # ------------------------------------------

        voice_profile = self.voice_selector.select(
            language
        )

        # ------------------------------------------
        # Analysis
        # ------------------------------------------

        analysis = self.speech_analyzer.analyze(
            presentation,
            language
        )

        # ------------------------------------------
        # Timing
        # ------------------------------------------

        analysis = self.timing_calculator.calculate(
            analysis
        )

        # ------------------------------------------
        # Final Speech Profile
        # ------------------------------------------

        speech_profile = self.speech_optimizer.optimize(
            analysis,
            voice_profile
        )

        logger.info(
            "Speech Profile generated successfully."
        )

        return speech_profile

"""
=========================================================
Edge TTS Provider
---------------------------------------------------------
Speech AI Platform

Implementação do Provider utilizando Microsoft Edge TTS.

Responsabilidades
-----------------
- Gerar áudio utilizando Microsoft Edge TTS
- Expor informações do Provider
- Encapsular toda a implementação do Edge
- Preparar suporte futuro para SSML

Author: Rodrigo Magalhães
=========================================================
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import logging
from pathlib import Path

import edge_tts

from .base_provider import BaseTTSProvider
from .provider_info import ProviderInfo

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class EdgeProvider(BaseTTSProvider):
    """
    Provider Microsoft Edge TTS.
    """

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def __init__(
        self,
        voice: str,
        rate: str,
        pitch: str,
        volume: str
    ):

        super().__init__(
            voice=voice,
            rate=rate,
            pitch=pitch,
            volume=volume
        )

        self.info = ProviderInfo(

            provider_id="edge",

            name="Microsoft Edge TTS",

            vendor="Microsoft",

            version="1.1.0",

            description="Microsoft Neural Text-to-Speech Provider",

            supports_ssml=False,

            supports_streaming=False,

            supports_multivoice=False,

            supports_neural=True,

            supported_languages=[

                "pt-BR",

                "en-US",

                "es-ES",

                "fr-FR",

                "de-DE",

                "it-IT",

                "ja-JP"

            ],

            supported_formats=[

                "mp3"

            ],

            max_characters=100000

        )

        logger.info(

            "Provider initialized: %s",

            self.info

        )

    # -------------------------------------------------

    async def _generate_async(

        self,

        text: str,

        output_path: Path

    ) -> None:

        logger.info("Generating audio...")

        logger.info("Voice.....: %s", self.voice)

        logger.info("Rate......: %s", self.rate)

        logger.info("Pitch.....: %s", self.pitch)

        logger.info("Volume....: %s", self.volume)

        communicate = edge_tts.Communicate(

            text=text,

            voice=self.voice,

            rate=self.rate,

            pitch=self.pitch,

            volume=self.volume

        )

        await communicate.save(

            str(output_path)

        )

        logger.info("Audio generated successfully.")

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def generate(

        self,

        text: str,

        output_path: Path

    ) -> Path:

        """
        Gera um arquivo de áudio.
        """

        output_path = Path(output_path)

        output_path.parent.mkdir(

            parents=True,

            exist_ok=True

        )

        asyncio.run(

            self._generate_async(

                text=text,

                output_path=output_path

            )

        )

        return output_path

    # -------------------------------------------------

    @property
    def provider_info(self) -> ProviderInfo:
        """
        Retorna os metadados do Provider.
        """

        return self.info

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def supports_language(
        self,
        locale: str
    ) -> bool:

        return locale in self.info.supported_languages

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def __str__(self):

        return str(self.info)

"""
=========================================================
Base TTS Provider

Contrato para qualquer mecanismo de geração de áudio.

Todo Provider deverá implementar esta interface.

Author: Rodrigo Magalhães
=========================================================
"""

from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
from pathlib import Path


class BaseTTSProvider(ABC):

    def __init__(
        self,
        voice: str,
        rate: str,
        pitch: str,
        volume: str
    ):

        self.voice = voice
        self.rate = rate
        self.pitch = pitch
        self.volume = volume

    # -------------------------------------------------

    @abstractmethod
    def generate(
        self,
        text: str,
        output_path: Path
    ) -> Path:
        """
        Gera o áudio utilizando o provider.

        Deve retornar o caminho do arquivo gerado.
        """
        pass

"""
=========================================================
Provider Loader

Responsável por descobrir automaticamente todos os
Providers disponíveis no pacote.

Author: Rodrigo Magalhães
=========================================================
"""

from importlib import import_module
from pathlib import Path

from .provider_registry import ProviderRegistry
from .base_provider import BaseTTSProvider


class ProviderLoader:

    @staticmethod
    def load():

        providers_path = Path(__file__).parent

        for file in providers_path.glob("*_provider.py"):

            if file.stem == "base_provider":
                continue

            module = import_module(
                f"providers.{file.stem}"
            )

            for obj in module.__dict__.values():

                if (
                    isinstance(obj, type)
                    and issubclass(obj, BaseTTSProvider)
                    and obj is not BaseTTSProvider
                ):

                    provider_id = (
                        obj.__name__
                        .replace("Provider", "")
                        .lower()
                    )

                    ProviderRegistry.register(
                        provider_id,
                        obj
                    )

"""
=========================================================
Provider Registry
---------------------------------------------------------
Speech AI Platform

Registro central de Providers TTS.

Responsabilidades
-----------------
- Registrar Providers
- Recuperar Providers
- Listar Providers disponíveis
- Permitir expansão dinâmica da plataforma

Author: Rodrigo Magalhães
=========================================================
"""

from typing import Type

from .base_provider import BaseTTSProvider


class ProviderRegistry:
    """
    Registro central dos Providers disponíveis.
    """

    _providers: dict[str, Type[BaseTTSProvider]] = {}

    # -------------------------------------------------

    @classmethod
    def register(
        cls,
        provider_id: str,
        provider_class: Type[BaseTTSProvider]
    ) -> None:
        """
        Registra um Provider.
        """

        cls._providers[provider_id.lower()] = provider_class

    # -------------------------------------------------

    @classmethod
    def get(
        cls,
        provider_id: str
    ) -> Type[BaseTTSProvider]:
        """
        Recupera a classe de um Provider.
        """

        provider = cls._providers.get(provider_id.lower())

        if provider is None:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Provider '{provider_id}' not registered."
            )

        return provider

    # -------------------------------------------------

    @classmethod
    def exists(
        cls,
        provider_id: str
    ) -> bool:
        """
        Verifica se o Provider está registrado.
        """

        return provider_id.lower() in cls._providers

    # -------------------------------------------------

    @classmethod
    def list(cls) -> list[str]:
        """
        Lista os Providers registrados.
        """

        return sorted(cls._providers.keys())

    # -------------------------------------------------

    @classmethod
    def clear(cls):
        """
        Limpa todos os registros.

        Utilizado apenas em testes.
        """

        cls._providers.clear()

"""
=========================================================
Provider Information
---------------------------------------------------------
Speech AI Platform

Representa os metadados e capacidades de um Provider TTS.

Este objeto não possui lógica de negócio.

Ele apenas descreve o Provider.

Author: Rodrigo Magalhães
=========================================================
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass, field


@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class ProviderInfo:
    """
    Metadados de um Provider TTS.

    Attributes
    ----------
    provider_id
        Identificador único do provider.

    name
        Nome amigável.

    vendor
        Fabricante da tecnologia.

    version
        Versão do provider.

    description
        Pequena descrição.

    supports_ssml
        Informa se aceita SSML.

    supports_streaming
        Informa se suporta streaming.

    supports_multivoice
        Suporta múltiplas vozes.

    supports_neural
        Utiliza vozes neurais.

    supported_languages
        Lista de idiomas suportados.

    supported_formats
        Lista de formatos de saída.

    max_characters
        Quantidade máxima de caracteres por requisição.
    """

    # -------------------------------------------------
    # Identificação
    # -------------------------------------------------

    provider_id: str

    name: str

    vendor: str

    version: str

    description: str

    # -------------------------------------------------
    # Capacidades
    # -------------------------------------------------

    supports_ssml: bool = False

    supports_streaming: bool = False

    supports_multivoice: bool = False

    supports_neural: bool = True

    # -------------------------------------------------
    # Idiomas e formatos
    # -------------------------------------------------

    supported_languages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)

    supported_formats: list[str] = field(default_factory=lambda: ["mp3"])

    # -------------------------------------------------
    # Limites
    # -------------------------------------------------

    max_characters: int = 100000

    # -------------------------------------------------

    @property
    def supports_multiple_languages(self) -> bool:
        """
        Retorna True caso suporte mais de um idioma.
        """

        return len(self.supported_languages) > 1

    # -------------------------------------------------

    @property
    def language_count(self) -> int:
        """
        Quantidade de idiomas suportados.
        """

        return len(self.supported_languages)

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        """
        Serializa os metadados para dicionário.

        Ideal para APIs REST ou exportação JSON.
        """

        return {
            "provider_id": self.provider_id,
            "name": self.name,
            "vendor": self.vendor,
            "version": self.version,
            "description": self.description,
            "supports_ssml": self.supports_ssml,
            "supports_streaming": self.supports_streaming,
            "supports_multivoice": self.supports_multivoice,
            "supports_neural": self.supports_neural,
            "supported_languages": self.supported_languages,
            "supported_formats": self.supported_formats,
            "max_characters": self.max_characters,
        }

    # -------------------------------------------------

    def __str__(self) -> str:
        """
        Representação amigável do Provider.
        """

        return (
            f"{self.name} "
            f"({self.vendor}) "
            f"v{self.version}"
        )

"""
=========================================================
Provider Factory
---------------------------------------------------------
Speech AI Platform

Factory responsável por instanciar Providers TTS.

Suporta:

- VoiceProfile (compatibilidade)
- SpeechProfile (nova arquitetura)

Author: Rodrigo Magalhães
=========================================================
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from config.config_manager import ConfigManager

from models.voice_profile import VoiceProfile
from models.speech_profile import SpeechProfile

from providers import ProviderRegistry


class ProviderFactory:

    # -------------------------------------------------

    @staticmethod
    def create(cfg: ConfigManager):
        """
        Método legado utilizado por partes antigas do projeto.
        """

        provider_class = ProviderRegistry.get(
            cfg.provider.lower()
        )

        return provider_class(
            voice=cfg.voice,
            rate=cfg.rate,
            pitch=cfg.pitch,
            volume=cfg.volume
        )

    # -------------------------------------------------

    @staticmethod
    def create_from_profile(
        profile: VoiceProfile
    ):
        """
        Compatibilidade com a Sprint 8.5
        """

        provider_class = ProviderRegistry.get(
            profile.provider.lower()
        )

        return provider_class(
            voice=profile.voice,
            rate=profile.rate,
            pitch=profile.pitch,
            volume=profile.volume
        )

    # -------------------------------------------------

    @staticmethod
    def create_from_speech_profile(
        profile: SpeechProfile
    ):
        """
        Nova arquitetura (Sprint 8.6).

        Cria um Provider utilizando um SpeechProfile.
        """

        provider_class = ProviderRegistry.get(
            profile.provider.lower()
        )

        return provider_class(
            voice=profile.voice,
            rate=profile.rate,
            pitch=profile.pitch,
            volume=profile.volume
        )

# Agentic AI: When Artificial Intelligence Stops Waiting and Starts Acting

Good morning, everyone.

I'd like to begin with a simple question.

**What if, tomorrow morning, you arrived at work and your first meeting had already been prepared?**

The presentation was created.

The market analysis was completed.

The competitors had been researched.

The financial risks had been identified.

The meeting invitations had been sent.

And all of this happened before you even opened your laptop.

This isn't science fiction.

It isn't a vision for the next decade.

It is happening now.

Welcome to the era of **Agentic Artificial Intelligence**.

For years, we've interacted with AI in a very simple way.

We asked a question.

AI gave us an answer.

We wrote a prompt.

AI generated content.

It was impressive.

It changed how we work.

But it was still reactive.

The human always had to initiate every step.

Today, that model is evolving.

We're moving from AI that **responds**...

to AI that **reasons**.

From AI that **creates**...

to AI that **acts**.

This is the difference between having a search engine...

and having a trusted colleague.

Imagine asking someone,

*"Help me launch a new product."*

A traditional AI might generate a marketing plan.

An Agentic AI asks a completely different question:

*"What is the objective?"*

Then it begins working.

It researches the market.

Analyzes competitors.

Creates financial projections.

Builds a project plan.

Identifies potential risks.

Schedules meetings.

Creates presentations.

Coordinates specialists.

And continuously adapts as new information becomes available.

It doesn't simply answer a request.

It pursues a goal.

That changes everything.

Because businesses are not built on isolated tasks.

Businesses are built on workflows.

On collaboration.

On decisions.

On thousands of interconnected activities that happen every single day.

And that's exactly where Agentic AI creates value.

Think about healthcare.

An intelligent agent can help physicians review patient histories, analyze laboratory results, identify treatment guidelines, coordinate appointments, and prepare clinical documentation.

Not to replace doctors...

but to give them more time to care for patients.

Think about manufacturing.

An AI agent can monitor production lines, predict equipment failures before they happen, optimize inventory, coordinate suppliers, and reduce operational costs.

Think about financial services.

Agents can detect fraud in real time, analyze investment opportunities, prepare compliance reports, and assist advisors in serving customers faster and more accurately.

This is no longer about automation.

It is about intelligent collaboration.

Another fascinating aspect of Agentic AI is that one agent rarely works alone.

We're entering a world of teams composed of digital specialists.

Imagine having a financial analyst...

a software engineer...

a cybersecurity expert...

a legal advisor...

and a project manager...

working together 24 hours a day.

Not as people.

But as specialized AI agents collaborating toward a common objective.

This concept is known as **multi-agent systems**.

Instead of one powerful model trying to solve every problem, organizations build networks of specialized agents that communicate, delegate tasks, verify each other's work, and deliver better outcomes together.

It begins to look surprisingly similar to how successful human organizations operate.

But with incredible speed.

However...

With greater capability comes greater responsibility.

If AI can make decisions...

access enterprise systems...

communicate with customers...

and execute business processes...

then governance becomes essential.

Trust becomes essential.

Security becomes essential.

Transparency becomes essential.

The future will not belong to organizations that simply deploy AI.

It will belong to organizations that deploy AI responsibly.

Human judgment will remain indispensable.

Ethics will remain indispensable.

Leadership will become even more important.

Because the real challenge isn't building intelligent machines.

The real challenge is building intelligent organizations.

Organizations where humans and AI complement one another.

Where repetitive work disappears.

Where creativity expands.

Where innovation accelerates.

Throughout history, every major technological revolution has changed the skills people needed.

The Industrial Revolution rewarded mechanical skills.

The Digital Revolution rewarded computer skills.

The AI Revolution will reward something even more valuable.

The ability to collaborate with intelligence itself.

The professionals who succeed won't necessarily be the ones who know the most about AI.

They will be the ones who know how to ask the right questions...

define clear objectives...

evaluate results critically...

and make better decisions alongside intelligent agents.

That is a profound shift.

Because AI is no longer just becoming another software tool.

It is becoming a teammate.

And perhaps that's the most exciting idea of all.

The future isn't about humans versus AI.

It isn't even about humans using AI.

The future is about humans working **with** AI.

Together.

Each contributing what they do best.

Machines bringing speed, scale, and consistency.

People bringing creativity, empathy, ethics, and vision.

That partnership has the potential to redefine every industry represented in this room.

We're standing at the beginning of one of the most significant technological transformations of our lifetime.

The organizations that start experimenting today...

learning today...

and building responsibly today...

will become the leaders of tomorrow.

So I'd like to leave you with one final thought.

The question is no longer,

**"Will AI change the way we work?"**

It already has.

The real question is,

**"When every company has access to intelligent agents... what will make yours different?"**

The answer won't be the technology.

The answer will be the people who know how to lead it.

Thank you.

SpeechAI Enterprise 2.0
A Plataforma de Inteligência de Fala construída para a nova geração da Inteligência Artificial

Vivemos uma nova era da Inteligência Artificial. Empresas do mundo inteiro estão automatizando processos, produzindo conteúdos e criando experiências digitais utilizando voz sintetizada. Entretanto, a maioria das soluções disponíveis ainda apresenta limitações significativas: dependência de serviços em nuvem, custos recorrentes por token, restrições de uso, preocupações com privacidade e pouca inteligência na forma como interpretam o conteúdo.

O SpeechAI Enterprise 2.0 nasceu para resolver esse problema.

Mais do que um mecanismo de Text-to-Speech, o SpeechAI é uma plataforma completa de Speech Intelligence capaz de compreender o conteúdo antes de transformá-lo em voz. Em vez de simplesmente ler um texto, a plataforma analisa idioma, estrutura linguística, complexidade, ritmo, pausas, estilo de leitura e contexto, produzindo uma narração muito mais natural, consistente e profissional.

Sua arquitetura foi desenvolvida seguindo princípios Enterprise, com componentes desacoplados, arquitetura baseada em Providers, pipeline modular, serviços especializados e modelos de domínio independentes. Essa abordagem permite que novos recursos sejam incorporados continuamente sem comprometer a estabilidade da plataforma.

O diferencial mais importante da versão 2.0 é a adoção do conceito Local AI First.

Enquanto grande parte do mercado depende de modelos hospedados em nuvem e cobrança por tokens, o SpeechAI foi projetado para executar modelos de Inteligência Artificial localmente. Isso significa que empresas podem utilizar modelos SLM (Small Language Models) executando em sua própria infraestrutura, eliminando custos recorrentes com APIs externas e garantindo total controle sobre dados sensíveis.

Essa estratégia oferece benefícios que vão muito além da redução de custos.

A execução local proporciona privacidade, baixa latência, funcionamento offline, independência de fornecedores e previsibilidade financeira. Organizações deixam de depender da disponibilidade de serviços externos e passam a controlar completamente o ciclo de vida da solução.

A arquitetura do SpeechAI também é agnóstica em relação aos modelos de IA utilizados. A plataforma pode trabalhar com modelos executados via Ollama, llama.cpp, Microsoft Phi-3, Mistral, Llama e futuros modelos especializados treinados especificamente para síntese de voz corporativa. Essa flexibilidade protege o investimento realizado hoje e garante que a solução continue evoluindo conforme novas tecnologias surgirem.

Outro diferencial importante é a separação entre inteligência determinística e inteligência generativa.

O SpeechAI utiliza um pipeline híbrido. Regras determinísticas realizam toda a análise estrutural do texto — identificando frases, cláusulas, conectivos, pausas e blocos de narração — enquanto modelos de IA atuam apenas onde realmente agregam valor, como compreensão semântica, identificação de intenção comunicacional, análise emocional e otimização do estilo de fala.

Esse modelo híbrido reduz drasticamente o consumo computacional, aumenta a previsibilidade dos resultados e mantém elevada qualidade na geração do áudio.

A plataforma foi concebida para ser extensível desde sua fundação. Novos motores de síntese podem ser adicionados através da arquitetura de Providers sem necessidade de alterações no restante do sistema. Da mesma forma, novos modelos de Inteligência Artificial podem ser incorporados simplesmente adicionando novos conectores à camada SLM Gateway.

Essa independência tecnológica torna o SpeechAI preparado para acompanhar a evolução da Inteligência Artificial pelos próximos anos.

Sua arquitetura também oferece recursos completos de observabilidade. Cada etapa do pipeline pode ser monitorada, registrada e auditada, permitindo compreender exatamente como cada narração foi construída. Métricas de qualidade, estatísticas linguísticas, tempo estimado, estratégia aplicada e parâmetros utilizados ficam disponíveis para análise e melhoria contínua.

Além do mercado corporativo, o SpeechAI possui aplicações em educação, treinamento, atendimento automatizado, criação de conteúdo, acessibilidade, comunicação institucional, plataformas de e-learning, geração automática de apresentações, assistentes virtuais e diversos outros cenários onde a qualidade da comunicação é um fator crítico.

Mais do que converter texto em áudio, o SpeechAI interpreta, compreende e decide a melhor forma de comunicar uma informação.

Esse é o verdadeiro conceito de Speech Intelligence.

A evolução para a versão 2.0 marca o início de uma nova etapa da plataforma. A Sprint 9 introduzirá uma camada completa de Inteligência Artificial Local responsável por compreender emoções, identificar intenção comunicacional, interpretar contexto semântico e otimizar automaticamente toda a estratégia de narração.

Com essa evolução, o SpeechAI deixa de ser apenas um sistema de síntese de voz para tornar-se uma plataforma cognitiva capaz de produzir narrações adaptativas, inteligentes e altamente naturais, preservando um dos princípios mais importantes da solução: independência tecnológica, controle total da infraestrutura e custo operacional previsível.

O SpeechAI Enterprise 2.0 representa uma nova geração de plataformas de voz corporativa. Uma arquitetura construída para organizações que desejam utilizar Inteligência Artificial de forma estratégica, escalável e sustentável, sem abrir mão da segurança, da privacidade e da autonomia sobre seus próprios dados.

SpeechAI Enterprise 2.0 não é apenas um software de Text-to-Speech.

É uma plataforma de Speech Intelligence, construída sobre uma arquitetura Enterprise, preparada para IA Local (SLM), com pipeline cognitivo próprio, zero dependência de tokens e foco em escalabilidade, governança e autonomia tecnológica.

Transformamos texto em comunicação inteligente.

from services.text_engines.lexical_analyzer import LexicalAnalyzer
from services.text_engines.clause_analyzer import ClauseAnalyzer
from services.text_engines.speech_block_builder import SpeechBlockBuilder
from services.text_engines.pause_planner import PausePlanner
from services.text_engines.ssml_engine import SSMLEngine

# load text
text = open("input/script.txt", encoding="utf-8").read()

# 1. Lexical
lex = LexicalAnalyzer()
tokens = lex.tokenize(text)

# 2. Clause
clause_engine = ClauseAnalyzer()
clauses = clause_engine.analyze(tokens)

# 3. Speech Blocks
builder = SpeechBlockBuilder()
blocks = builder.build(clauses)

# 4. Pause Planner
planner = PausePlanner()
clauses = planner.plan(clauses)

# 5. SSML
ssml_engine = SSMLEngine()
ssml = ssml_engine.build(clauses)

print("\n===== BLOCKS =====\n")
print(blocks)

print("\n===== SSML =====\n")
print(ssml)

# Agentic Artificial Intelligence: The Next Evolution of AI

Good morning everyone.

Today, I would like to talk about one of the most exciting topics in technology: **Agentic Artificial Intelligence**, also known as Agentic AI.

Over the last few years, Artificial Intelligence has transformed the way we work, communicate, and make decisions. We have seen AI generate text, create images, analyze data, write software, and answer complex questions. These capabilities have already changed many industries.

However, we are now entering a new phase.

Traditional AI is reactive. It waits for a user to ask a question before producing an answer. Agentic AI is different. Instead of simply responding to prompts, it can plan, reason, make decisions, use tools, collaborate with other systems, and execute complete workflows with minimal human intervention.

Think of the difference between a calculator and a personal assistant.

A calculator performs exactly one task when requested. A personal assistant understands your objectives, organizes activities, coordinates resources, and helps you achieve a desired outcome.

That is the promise of Agentic AI.

An AI agent begins by understanding a goal rather than a single instruction. It breaks that goal into smaller tasks, determines the best strategy, selects the appropriate tools, evaluates the results, and adapts its actions whenever necessary.

This approach allows organizations to automate not only repetitive tasks but also complex business processes that previously required human coordination.

Imagine an employee asking an AI agent to prepare a quarterly business report.

Instead of only generating text, the agent can collect information from multiple systems, analyze financial data, create visual dashboards, summarize key insights, prepare a presentation, schedule a meeting with stakeholders, and even suggest strategic recommendations.

The employee remains in control, but the AI dramatically increases productivity.

Another important characteristic of Agentic AI is collaboration.

Rather than relying on a single model, organizations are increasingly building ecosystems composed of multiple specialized agents. One agent may focus on customer service, another on financial analysis, another on cybersecurity, and another on software development.

Together, these agents communicate, exchange information, and coordinate their work to solve problems more efficiently than any individual system.

Of course, this new level of autonomy also introduces important responsibilities.

As AI becomes capable of making decisions and taking actions, organizations must establish strong governance, security, transparency, and ethical guidelines.

Human oversight remains essential.

Agentic AI should not replace human judgment. Instead, it should amplify human intelligence by allowing people to focus on creativity, innovation, strategic thinking, and meaningful interactions.

Technology alone is never enough.

Successful organizations will combine advanced AI capabilities with well-designed business processes, high-quality data, skilled professionals, and responsible governance.

This is why AI literacy is becoming as important as digital literacy was twenty years ago.

Every employee—not only software engineers or data scientists—will need to understand how to work effectively alongside intelligent agents.

The future workplace will not be defined by humans competing against AI.

Instead, it will be defined by humans collaborating with AI.

Those who learn how to delegate repetitive work to intelligent agents will gain more time for innovation, customer relationships, and solving the challenges that truly require human creativity.

We are not simply witnessing another technological improvement.

We are experiencing a fundamental shift in the relationship between people and technology.

Agentic AI represents the next generation of digital transformation.

Organizations that invest today in responsible AI, governance, education, and experimentation will be better prepared to lead tomorrow.

The question is no longer whether Agentic AI will transform our businesses.

The real question is: **How quickly are we prepared to embrace this transformation?**

Thank you very much for your time.

Slide 1 – AI Governance Overview


Hello everyone.

Good morning, good afternoon and good evening, depending on where you are joining us from.

Thank you very much for being here today.

My name is Rodrigo, Currently, I am responsible for AI projects in South America, supporting all business areas and their demands.

Today I would like to share how we are building AI Governance in South America.

Our region includes several countries and many different business areas.

Because of this diversity, we needed a simple governance model that allows innovation while maintaining security, compliance and business value.

Our vision is not only to develop AI solutions.

Our goal is to create an environment where AI can be used responsibly, efficiently and at scale.

Our governance is based on four main pillars.

The first pillar is Governance.
It defines how decisions are made.

The second pillar is AI Platforms and Tools.
It provides employees with approved technologies.

The third pillar is AI Culture and Knowledge.
It focuses on education and adoption.

Finally, the fourth pillar is AI Use Cases.
It helps transform business ideas into real solutions.

Together, these four pillars create one complete AI ecosystem.


Slide 2 – Organization Structure

This slide shows how our organization is structured.

AI Governance starts with executive sponsorship.

Our executive sponsors define priorities and support strategic decisions.

The AI Governance Manager coordinates all governance activities across South America.

We also have AI experts who provide technical guidance and support project implementation.

One important part of our model is the Business Ambassador network.

Business Ambassadors represent different business functions.

They understand local business challenges and identify opportunities where AI can create value.

Working together with them, we have our Functional ICT teams.

They evaluate technical feasibility and support solution development.

Another important connection is with the Global Center of Excellence for Generative AI.

This collaboration allows us to align regional initiatives with global standards, best practices and corporate strategy.

In summary, our model combines business, technology and governance into one collaborative structure.


Slide 3 – Governance Committees



Governance is not only about approving projects.

It is also about creating transparency, reducing risks and supporting innovation.

For this reason, we created three governance committees.

The first committee is the Executive Committee.

This committee meets every quarter.

Its main responsibility is strategic decision-making.

It reviews important AI initiatives, monitors business results and defines priorities for the region.

The second committee is the Operative Committee.

This committee meets every month.

It works much closer to day-to-day activities.

The team supports AI literacy, evaluates new opportunities, helps teams use the approved AI platforms and follows project execution.

It is also responsible for sharing successful initiatives across different business areas.

The third committee is the Regulatory Committee.

This committee focuses on responsible AI.

It evaluates privacy, security, legal requirements and ethical risks.

It also monitors changes in AI regulations in different countries.

The objective is to make sure AI solutions are compliant before they are implemented.

Together, these three committees provide strategic direction, operational support and regulatory oversight.


Slide 4 – Business Ambassadors Network


One of the biggest success factors of our governance model is our Business Ambassador network.

Instead of having AI managed only by the IT organization, we involve people from every business area.

These ambassadors help identify business problems that AI can solve.

They also encourage collaboration between departments.

Another important responsibility is promoting AI adoption inside their teams.

To support this community, we created a central AI portal.

This portal contains governance documentation, AI news, training materials, videos, available AI tools and useful references.

It has become the main communication channel for AI inside South America.

Our objective is to make AI knowledge available to everyone, regardless of their technical background.


Slide 5 – AI Community and Knowledge Sharing



Technology alone is not enough.

People are the key to successful AI adoption.

Because of this, we invest heavily in knowledge sharing.

During the year we organize AI events for all employees.

Internal AI Ambassadors present solutions already implemented inside Stellantis.

These presentations allow other teams to learn from successful projects.

We also invite external companies and technology partners.

They share market trends, new technologies and practical experiences.

These sessions are open to the entire organization.

They help create curiosity, inspire innovation and increase AI maturity across the region.

Every event also creates new networking opportunities between business and technology teams.


Slide 6 – AI Portfolio and Results



This final slide shows our AI portfolio.

Today, South America has more than 280 AI initiatives registered in our inventory.

These initiatives cover almost every business function, including Manufacturing, Supply Chain, Engineering, Finance, Human Resources and Sales.

Each initiative follows a governance process from idea generation to production.

Some projects are already delivering business value.

Others are currently under development, proof of concept or business evaluation.

We also classify projects into two categories.

The first category is Self-Service AI.

These are solutions that employees can safely create using approved AI platforms.

The second category is Non Self-Service AI.

These projects usually require software development, integration or more advanced governance.

Having one centralized inventory gives us complete visibility of our AI landscape.

It allows us to monitor progress, prioritize investments, measure business impact and identify opportunities for collaboration between different teams.

Most importantly, it helps us avoid duplicated efforts and accelerate innovation across the region.

Closing



To conclude,

AI Governance is much more than a process.

It is the foundation that allows innovation to happen in a safe, responsible and scalable way.

Our experience in South America shows that successful AI adoption depends on three key elements:

strong governance,
collaboration between business and technology,
and continuous learning.

We are still evolving, but we have already built a solid foundation that supports both local innovation and global alignment.

We hope our experience can also contribute to discussions across other regions.

Thank you very much for your attention.

It will be a pleasure to answer your questions.

Slide 1 – AI Governance Overview


Hello everyone.

Good morning, good afternoon and good evening, depending on where you are joining us from.

Thank you very much for being here today.

My name is Rodrigo, Currently, I am responsible for AI projects in South America, supporting all business areas and their demands.

Today I would like to share how we are building AI Governance in South America.

Our region includes several countries and many different business areas.

Because of this diversity, we needed a simple governance model that allows innovation while maintaining security, compliance and business value.

Our vision is not only to develop AI solutions.

Our goal is to create an environment where AI can be used responsibly, efficiently and at scale.

Our governance is based on four main pillars.

The first pillar is Governance.
It defines how decisions are made.

The second pillar is AI Platforms and Tools.
It provides employees with approved technologies.

The third pillar is AI Culture and Knowledge.
It focuses on education and adoption.

Finally, the fourth pillar is AI Use Cases.
It helps transform business ideas into real solutions.

Together, these four pillars create one complete AI ecosystem.


Slide 2 – Organization Structure

This slide shows how our organization is structured.

AI Governance starts with executive sponsorship.

Our executive sponsors define priorities and support strategic decisions.

The AI Governance Manager coordinates all governance activities across South America.

We also have AI experts who provide technical guidance and support project implementation.

One important part of our model is the Business Ambassador network.

Business Ambassadors represent different business functions.

They understand local business challenges and identify opportunities where AI can create value.

Working together with them, we have our Functional ICT teams.

They evaluate technical feasibility and support solution development.

Another important connection is with the Global Center of Excellence for Generative AI.

This collaboration allows us to align regional initiatives with global standards, best practices and corporate strategy.

In summary, our model combines business, technology and governance into one collaborative structure.


Slide 3 – Governance Committees

It is also about creating transparency, reducing risks and supporting innovation.

For this reason, we created three governance committees.

The first committee is the Executive Committee.

This committee meets every quarter.

Its main responsibility is strategic decision-making.

It reviews important AI initiatives, monitors business results and defines priorities for the region.

The second committee is the Operative Committee.

This committee meets every month.

It works much closer to day-to-day activities.

The team supports AI literacy, evaluates new opportunities, helps teams use the approved AI platforms and follows project execution.

It is also responsible for sharing successful initiatives across different business areas.
